methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $5.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Graphyte is a Series A climate and energy technology company currently valued at $200M post-money on $57M total raised. The valuation assessment relies on a comp universe of 8 private companies in the same sector and adjacent stage, supplemented by 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression approach applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $200M last post-money valuation, yielding $400M. The peer-median method examines 8 same-sector peers including Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), producing a median valuation of $305M. The peer range spans $110M to $5.0B, reflecting material dispersion in the climate-tech funding market.

These two methods triangulate to a range of $259M–$460M, with a median of $352M. The current $200M valuation sits below this range, suggesting moderate upside potential consistent with Series A progression norms. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation within the climate-energy sector, founder-specific risk factors, and the outsized influence of Commonwealth Fusion Systems on peer comps. The wide peer range ($110M–$5.0B) indicates significant heterogeneity in how the market values companies at this stage, limiting precision of any point estimate.
